xhcarina/superset#1.reasoning: The root cause is localized to a single useEffect in Tab.tsx that schedules a setTimeout-driven onRefresh without cancelling it on unmount/dep-change, and the same fork has already shipped PR #12 with this exact fix title, confirming a clean programmatic remedy. The change is small, well-scoped, and easy to cover with unit tests.
xhcarina/superset#1.action_plan:
1. Open superset-frontend/src/dashboard/components/gridComponents/Tab/Tab.tsx and inspect the `useEffect` at lines 173-213 that fires when `renderType === RENDER_TAB_CONTENT`, `isComponentVisible` is true, and `lastRefreshTime > tabActivationTime`; confirm that it schedules `dispatch(onRefresh(...))` via `setTimeout(..., CHART_MOUNT_DELAY)` with no cleanup, and that `handledRefreshRef` is a component-scoped ref that resets on remount.
2. Reproduce the loop on Superset 6.0.0 with a dashboard structured as tab > tab > chart: force-refresh the dashboard and confirm repeated `POST /api/v1/chart/data` for the inner chart while the outer and inner Tab components unmount/remount (tab switching, visibility toggles, and lazy load re-render during force refresh), which causes the pending setTimeout from a prior mount to fire against a new mount whose `handledRefreshRef` has been reset.
3. Add a `useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null>` (e.g. `pendingRefreshTimerRef`) inside Tab.tsx. Store the timer id returned by `setTimeout` in that ref when scheduling the refresh, and return a cleanup function from the `useEffect` that calls `clearTimeout(pendingRefreshTimerRef.current)` and nulls the ref so pending refreshes are cancelled when the Tab unmounts, becomes invisible, or when dependencies change before the 100 ms fires.
4. Harden the de-dup logic: before scheduling, bail out if `pendingRefreshTimerRef.current` is already set for the same `refreshKey`, and only set `handledRefreshRef.current = refreshKey` after the dispatch actually fires (inside the setTimeout callback) so a cancelled-then-remounted tab does not re-run the same refresh, and a newly mounted inner tab does not double-dispatch a refresh that the outer tab already queued.
5. Ensure the nested case does not double-dispatch: since `getChartIdsFromComponent(props.id, dashboardLayout)` returns all descendant chart ids, an outer TAB will already include the inner chart; guard with a check such as `props.component.type === TAB_TYPE` plus skipping the scheduling when the tab is not the deepest visible ancestor for those charts (e.g. by only scheduling when the tab's own children contain charts directly, or by coordinating via a shared ref / Redux slice keyed on `lastRefreshTime` so only the first tab to observe a given refresh key owns it).
6. Verify that `onRefresh(chartIds, true, 0, dashboardInfo.id, /* skipFiltersRefresh */ false, /* isLazyLoad */ true)` in actions/dashboardState.ts does not itself update `lastRefreshTime` (it should not when `isLazyLoad=true`); if it does, adjust the reducer so the lazy-load path leaves `lastRefreshTime` untouched, eliminating the feedback that re-triggers the effect.
7. Add Jest + React Testing Library unit tests in `Tab.test.tsx` covering: (a) scheduled refresh is cancelled when the Tab unmounts before CHART_MOUNT_DELAY, (b) a single force refresh on a nested-tab dashboard produces exactly one dispatched `onRefresh` per chart, (c) `handledRefreshRef` correctly suppresses duplicate dispatches within the same `lastRefreshTime`, and (d) remounting the tab after cancellation does not replay the cancelled refresh.
8. Run `npm run test -- Tab` and `npm run test -- TabsRenderer` in `superset-frontend/`, then `pre-commit run --all-files` per repo conventions, fixing any lint/type issues before opening a PR titled `fix(dashboard): cancel pending nested-tab refresh on unmount` with reproduction steps, Athena impact note, and before/after network traces.

xhcarina/superset#2.reasoning: Root cause is isolated and well-understood: on the Home page Chart table, the 'Other' (default) tab seeds its collection from pre-fetched props without ever calling fetchData, so lastFetchDataConfigRef is null and handleChartDelete's post-delete refreshData() is a no-op. The dashboard counterpart already solves this exact problem via a getData fallback, giving a clear, localized pattern to mirror.
xhcarina/superset#2.action_plan:
1. Reproduce and confirm: with LocalStorageKeys.HomepageChartFilter unset (defaults to TableTab.Other), on /superset/welcome/ delete a chart card via ChartCard's Delete menu; the API DELETE succeeds and a toast appears but the card stays because refreshData() in superset-frontend/src/views/CRUD/utils.tsx handleChartDelete hits the `if (lastFetchDataConfigRef.current)` branch with null (see superset-frontend/src/views/CRUD/hooks.ts refreshData) and bails out.
2. Extend handleChartDelete in superset-frontend/src/views/CRUD/utils.tsx to accept an optional `getData?: (tab: TableTab) => void` parameter (mirroring handleDashboardDelete) and, in the success handler, branch: if chartFilter === TableTab.Mine -> refreshData(filters); else if chartFilter === TableTab.Other && getData -> getData(TableTab.Other); else refreshData(). Keep the existing Mine filter behavior and error toast untouched.
3. Thread `getData` through the Home page: in superset-frontend/src/features/home/ChartTable.tsx pass the existing `getData` down to ChartCard as a new prop; in superset-frontend/src/features/charts/ChartCard.tsx add the prop to ChartCardProps and forward it as the 7th argument of handleChartDelete inside the ConfirmStatusChange onConfirm.
4. As a defensive follow-up, also ensure the home-page `useEffect` primes lastFetchDataConfigRef for non-Favorite tabs when needed (optional; the getData plumbing above already fixes the bug without this change, but consider calling getData once after initial mount when the prefetched data is empty so manual refresh works in edge cases).
5. Add/adjust tests: extend superset-frontend/src/features/home/ChartTable.test.tsx to simulate a delete on the Other tab and assert the chart list API (/api/v1/chart/?q=...) is re-fetched (via fetchMock) after the DELETE resolves; add a unit test for handleChartDelete covering the three branches (Mine -> refreshData(filters), Other with getData -> getData called, default -> refreshData()).
6. Run `npm run test -- ChartTable` and `npm run test -- utils` in superset-frontend, then `pre-commit run --files <changed files>` (prettier, eslint) per AGENTS.md before pushing.
7. Open a PR titled `fix(home): refresh chart list after deleting a chart on the home page` with a short SUMMARY, BEFORE/AFTER GIFs of deleting a chart on the Home page's Other tab, and TESTING INSTRUCTIONS referencing the original apache/superset#39428 issue.

xhcarina/superset#4.reasoning: The root cause is clearly localized to lines 74-89 of superset/app.py, which manually prefix APP_ICON and theme tokens that the frontend's ensureStaticPrefix/ensureAppRoot already handle; removing these lines is a minimal, contained change with existing frontend tests in Menu.test.tsx covering the relevant behavior. Slight uncertainty remains around brandLogoHref — the issue claims it still needs manual prefixing, but ensureAppRoot in pathUtils.ts already prefixes relative paths with applicationRoot(), so that path would likely also double-prefix; a small verification in Menu.tsx/pathUtils.ts resolves this easily.
xhcarina/superset#4.action_plan: 1. In superset/app.py, remove the manual APP_ICON prefixing block (lines 74-79): delete the `if app.config.get("APP_ICON", "").startswith("/static/") and app_root != "/"` branch that reassigns `app.config["APP_ICON"] = f"{app_root}{app.config['APP_ICON']}"`. 2. In the same block, remove the theme-token loop that reassigns `token["brandLogoUrl"] = f"{app_root}{token['brandLogoUrl']}"` for THEME_DEFAULT and THEME_DARK (lines 80-86), since ensureStaticPrefix() in superset-frontend/src/utils/assetUrl.ts already prepends STATIC_ASSETS_PREFIX (which is set to app_root on line 73) to any path starting with `/`. 3. Decide on brandLogoHref handling: verify that Menu.tsx calls `ensureAppRoot(theme.brandLogoHref)` (line 295) and that `ensureAppRoot` in superset-frontend/src/utils/pathUtils.ts already prefixes relative paths with applicationRoot(); if confirmed, also remove the `token["brandLogoHref"] = app_root` assignment (lines 87-89) from app.py to avoid double-prefix, keeping the default `"/"` which ensureAppRoot will correctly expand to app_root on the frontend. 4. Keep the `STATIC_ASSETS_PREFIX = app_root` default (line 73) and the `APPLICATION_ROOT` assignment (lines 90-91) — those are correct and load-bearing. 5. Add/adjust unit tests: create a test in tests/unit_tests/ covering create_app() with SUPERSET_APP_ROOT=/dashboards, asserting that APP_ICON, THEME_DEFAULT.token.brandLogoUrl, and THEME_DARK.token.brandLogoUrl are NOT mutated (remain at their original `/static/...` values), and that STATIC_ASSETS_PREFIX equals `/dashboards`. 6. Extend superset-frontend/src/features/home/Menu.test.tsx (which already mocks staticAssetsPrefix and applicationRoot) with a regression case: given staticAssetsPrefix='/dashboards' and a raw `/static/assets/images/...` brandLogoUrl, assert the rendered `` equals exactly `/dashboards/static/assets/images/...` (single prefix). 7. Run `pre-commit run --all-files` (mypy, ruff, prettier, eslint) and the affected Jest tests (`npm run test -- Menu.test.tsx assetUrl.test.ts`) plus the new pytest unit test. 8. Update UPDATING.md noting the behavior change for deployments that relied on the (buggy) double-prefix workaround, and reference the companion issues #39429 and #39431 in the PR description. 9. Manually verify in a subdirectory deployment (SUPERSET_APP_ROOT=/dashboards) that the logo loads from `/dashboards/static/assets/images/superset-logo-horiz.png` and that the brand link navigates to `/dashboards`. 10. Open a PR titled `fix(app_root): stop double-prefixing logo and brand URLs in subdirectory deploys` following the repo's PR template.
